title: Microchilus repens
taxonomy: Microchilus repens is a species within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants, known as angiosperms. More specifically, Microchilus repens is a member of the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Microchileae. The genus Microchilus is often included in the subtribe Microchiliinae. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships among other orchids.

intro: Microchilus repens is a climbing orchid plant native to tropical regions, characterized by its delicate white flowers and slender stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Microchilus repens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
